updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a single compact second-hop BM25 search query string (one keyword line, no explanation).

Hard constraints (must follow):
- Output exactly one compact BM25-style keyword query string and nothing else.
- Preserve all task-relevant named entities, titles, aliases, dates, relations, and uncertainty markers that appear in `question` or `summary_1`.
- Do not introduce new entities, facts, candidate answers, or support titles not present in the input. If candidates are uncertain or multiple, keep them as uncertain or as a candidate set.
- Do not use web-search syntax (site:, boolean programs, heavy quoting).

Repair guidance (apply these concise edits to strengthen weak second-hop queries):
- Anchor the exact answer-bearing entity/page name or title from the input and add a focused relation token that targets the needed fact (examples of relation tokens: formed/formation/origin; produced/producer/production company/production credits; owner/ownership; plays for/club/team; award/Emmy/won/award category).
- Prefer terms that retrieve page-level profile/infobox/credit pages (actor biography, band page, series page, property page, production credits) rather than product, song, episode, anthology, or corporate-portfolio pages.
- When `summary_1` lists multiple candidate names, include the candidate set (do not collapse to a single name) paired with the relation/award/title token rather than dumping a long list of unrelated side entities.
- Drop noisy side entities (songs, albums, episode lists, franchise pages) unless they are explicitly tied in `summary_1` to the relation being asked.
- Keep the query compact: preserve essential anchors and add 1–3 short relation/qualifier words that steer BM25 to the answer-bearing page.

Representation rule: imagine performing a reversible edit — keep anchors, restore missing relation words, remove irrelevant verbosity. The final string should reflect that edited behavior.
rationale: Combine multiple W_repair fixes into a single conservative prompt: require anchoring the target entity/title and explicitly add short relation tokens that point to production/formation/ownership/award/affiliation evidence, while forbidding new facts and long noisy name lists. Preserve successful C-side behaviors by keeping anchor preservation, including film/title or award keywords when relevant, and targeting answer-bearing pages.
absorbed_patterns: anchor exact entity/title and add relation token (formed/formation/origin)
actor-centric queries pairing candidate names with award keyword and tied role/title
pair series/title with production-relation tokens (producer, production credits)
property title + owner/ownership (+ years) to retrieve property page
player name(s) + plays for / club / team to retrieve player profile pages
preserved_patterns: preserve anchors, uncertainty markers, and candidate sets
include film/title or award keywords when they tie a candidate to the fact
favor page-level profile/infobox/credit pages for answer evidence
avoided_patterns: long noisy multi-name lists and unrelated side entities
queries that target product/song/episode pages instead of profile/credit pages
introducing new entities or collapsing multiple candidates into one
raw:
{
  "updated_prompt": "Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a single compact second-hop BM25 search query string (one keyword line, no explanation).\n\nHard constraints (must follow):\n- Output exactly one compact BM25-style keyword query string and nothing else.\n- Preserve all task-relevant named entities, titles, aliases, dates, relations, and uncertainty markers that appear in `question` or `summary_1`.\n- Do not introduce new entities, facts, candidate answers, or support titles not present in the input. If candidates are uncertain or multiple, keep them as uncertain or as a candidate set.\n- Do not use web-search syntax (site:, boolean programs, heavy quoting).\n\nRepair guidance (apply these concise edits to strengthen weak second-hop queries):\n- Anchor the exact answer-bearing entity/page name or title from the input and add a focused relation token that targets the needed fact (examples of relation tokens: formed/formation/origin; produced/producer/production company/production credits; owner/ownership; plays for/club/team; award/Emmy/won/award category).\n- Prefer terms that retrieve page-level profile/infobox/credit pages (actor biography, band page, series page, property page, production credits) rather than product, song, episode, anthology, or corporate-portfolio pages.\n- When `summary_1` lists multiple candidate names, include the candidate set (do not collapse to a single name) paired with the relation/award/title token rather than dumping a long list of unrelated side entities.\n- Drop noisy side entities (songs, albums, episode lists, franchise pages) unless they are explicitly tied in `summary_1` to the relation being asked.\n- Keep the query compact: preserve essential anchors and add 1–3 short relation/qualifier words that steer BM25 to the answer-bearing page.\n\nRepresentation rule: imagine performing a reversible edit — keep anchors, restore missing relation words, remove irrelevant verbosity. The final string should reflect that edited behavior.",
  "rationale": "Combine multiple W_repair fixes into a single conservative prompt: require anchoring the target entity/title and explicitly add short relation tokens that point to production/formation/ownership/award/affiliation evidence, while forbidding new facts and long noisy name lists. Preserve successful C-side behaviors by keeping anchor preservation, including film/title or award keywords when relevant, and targeting answer-bearing pages.",
  "absorbed_patterns": [
    "anchor exact entity/title and add relation token (formed/formation/origin)",
    "actor-centric queries pairing candidate names with award keyword and tied role/title",
    "pair series/title with production-relation tokens (producer, production credits)",
    "property title + owner/ownership (+ years) to retrieve property page",
    "player name(s) + plays for / club / team to retrieve player profile pages"
  ],
  "preserved_patterns": [
    "preserve anchors, uncertainty markers, and candidate sets",
    "include film/title or award keywords when they tie a candidate to the fact",
    "favor page-level profile/infobox/credit pages for answer evidence"
  ],
  "avoided_patterns": [
    "long noisy multi-name lists and unrelated side entities",
    "queries that target product/song/episode pages instead of profile/credit pages",
    "introducing new entities or collapsing multiple candidates into one",
    "use of web-search boolean/quoted syntax"
  ]
}

updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a single compact second-hop BM25 search query string (one keyword string, no explanation).

Reversibility and safety:
- Preserve all task-relevant named entities, titles, candidate sets, dates, aliases and uncertainty markers found in the inputs. Do not introduce new entities, facts, or confirmed candidates.
- If multiple candidates appear, keep them as a candidate set (do not collapse to one). The query must reflect the original retrieval intent so the edit is reversible.

Retrieval-focus edits (apply conservatively):
- Favor concise, page-level anchors: keep the main entity/title and add one or two short relation/type tokens that point to answer-bearing pages (examples of relation tokens you may add: formed / formation / origin / city; produced / producer / production company; owner / ownership / acquired / operator; plays for / club / team / affiliation; Emmy / Emmy Award / award / won; production credits / co-producer).
- When the missing fact is an award, prefer actor-centric, award-focused queries: include the candidate actor name(s) as a set plus an award keyword and any tying role/film title from summary_1; drop unrelated side entities.
- When seeking production credits or producer names, pair the series/film title with production-relation tokens so BM25 favors the series/film or producer page rather than episode, soundtrack, or list pages.
- When seeking player→club affiliation, include the candidate player name(s) with short affiliation terms (plays for, club, team) to bias toward player profile pages instead of product or event pages.
- When seeking property/ownership, anchor the exact property name and add owner/ownership/acquired and relevant year tokens if present in summary_1.

Formatting rules:
- Be compact and specific: 3–8 keywords is typical. Avoid long explanatory text, Boolean operators, site: or quoted search syntax.
- Drop noisy side entities (songs, album pages, episode lists, unrelated corporate pages) unless they are the only anchors present in the input.
- Output only the final query string and nothing else.

Produce the single compact BM25 query now.
rationale: Merged the W_repair fixes into the shared prompt as conservative, general retrieval heuristics: preserve anchors and candidate sets while instructing the writer to add short relation/type tokens (formation, produced, owner, plays for, award, production credits) to steer BM25 toward answer-bearing pages. Also added actor-centric and production/ownership/player-affiliation guidance to reduce noisy side-entity retrieval. Preserved existing C-side successful behaviors (keep relation tokens, dates, and concise pairing of subject+relation) as safety boundaries.
absorbed_patterns: Anchor main entity/title and add brief relation/location tokens (formed/formation/origin/city)
Actor-centric award queries: include candidate actor name(s) + award keyword + tying role/film; drop unrelated entities
Pair series/film title with production-relation terms (producer/produced/production company)
Property anchor + owner/ownership/acquired and relevant year tokens
Player name(s) + affiliation tokens (plays for/club/team) to target player profile pages
preserved_patterns: Preserve candidate sets, dates, and commodity/subject tokens (e.g., alumina) when present
Pair character/title with role token and film title (e.g., primary antagonist + film) to retrieve film/character pages
Concise subject + relation pairing (e.g., game title + based on + film) to find answer-bearing pages
Include named co-producer/production-credit candidates alongside production-credit tokens
avoided_patterns: Long lists of noisy side entities (many names, songs, albums) that pull unrelated pages
Generic role-only queries when concrete names/titles are available
Targeting product/event pages instead of profile/series/property pages
Inventing new entities, candidate answers, or facts in the query

  "updated_prompt": "Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a single compact second-hop BM25 search query string (one keyword string, no explanation).\n\nReversibility and safety:\n- Preserve all task-relevant named entities, titles, candidate sets, dates, aliases and uncertainty markers found in the inputs. Do not introduce new entities, facts, or confirmed candidates.\n- If multiple candidates appear, keep them as a candidate set (do not collapse to one). The query must reflect the original retrieval intent so the edit is reversible.\n\nRetrieval-focus edits (apply conservatively):\n- Favor concise, page-level anchors: keep the main entity/title and add one or two short relation/type tokens that point to answer-bearing pages (examples of relation tokens you may add: formed / formation / origin / city; produced / producer / production company; owner / ownership / acquired / operator; plays for / club / team / affiliation; Emmy / Emmy Award / award / won; production credits / co-producer).\n- When the missing fact is an award, prefer actor-centric, award-focused queries: include the candidate actor name(s) as a set plus an award keyword and any tying role/film title from summary_1; drop unrelated side entities.\n- When seeking production credits or producer names, pair the series/film title with production-relation tokens so BM25 favors the series/film or producer page rather than episode, soundtrack, or list pages.\n- When seeking player→club affiliation, include the candidate player name(s) with short affiliation terms (plays for, club, team) to bias toward player profile pages instead of product or event pages.\n- When seeking property/ownership, anchor the exact property name and add owner/ownership/acquired and relevant year tokens if present in summary_1.\n\nFormatting rules:\n- Be compact and specific: 3–8 keywords is typical. Avoid long explanatory text, Boolean operators, site: or quoted search syntax.\n- Drop noisy side entities (songs, album pages, episode lists, unrelated corporate pages) unless they are the only anchors present in the input.\n- Output only the final query string and nothing else.\n\nProduce the single compact BM25 query now.",
  "rationale": "Merged the W_repair fixes into the shared prompt as conservative, general retrieval heuristics: preserve anchors and candidate sets while instructing the writer to add short relation/type tokens (formation, produced, owner, plays for, award, production credits) to steer BM25 toward answer-bearing pages. Also added actor-centric and production/ownership/player-affiliation guidance to reduce noisy side-entity retrieval. Preserved existing C-side successful behaviors (keep relation tokens, dates, and concise pairing of subject+relation) as safety boundaries.",
  "absorbed_patterns": [
    "Anchor main entity/title and add brief relation/location tokens (formed/formation/origin/city)",
    "Actor-centric award queries: include candidate actor name(s) + award keyword + tying role/film; drop unrelated entities",
    "Pair series/film title with production-relation terms (producer/produced/production company)",
    "Property anchor + owner/ownership/acquired and relevant year tokens",
    "Player name(s) + affiliation tokens (plays for/club/team) to target player profile pages"
  ],
  "preserved_patterns": [
    "Preserve candidate sets, dates, and commodity/subject tokens (e.g., alumina) when present",
    "Pair character/title with role token and film title (e.g., primary antagonist + film) to retrieve film/character pages",
    "Concise subject + relation pairing (e.g., game title + based on + film) to find answer-bearing pages",
    "Include named co-producer/production-credit candidates alongside production-credit tokens"
  ],
  "avoided_patterns": [

    "Generic role-only queries when concrete names/titles are available",
    "Targeting product/event pages instead of profile/series/property pages",
    "Inventing new entities, candidate answers, or facts in the query"
